NOTE: Right now we allow a DNS cache to be shared between multiple clusters. Though we have\\n247:     // connection/request circuit breakers on the cluster, we don't have any way to control the\\n248:     // maximum hosts on a cluster. We currently assume that host data shared via shared pointer is\\n249:     // a marginal memory cost above that already used by connections and requests, so relying on\\n250:     // connection/request circuit breakers is sufficient. We may have to revisit this in the\\n251:     // future.\\n252:     const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\\n253:     if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\\n254:       // If we only have an address change, we can do that swap inline without any other updates.\\n255:       // The appropriate R/W locking is in place to allow this. The details of this locking are:\\n256:       //  - Hosts are not thread local, they are global.\\n257:       //  - We take a read lock when reading the address and a write lock when changing it.\\n258:       //  - Address updates are very rare.\\n259:       //  - Address reads are only done when a connection is being made and a \\\"real\\\" host\\n260:       //    description is created or the host is queried via the admin endpoint. Both of\\n261:       //    these operations are relatively rare and the read lock is held for a short period\\n262:       //    of time.\\n263:       //\\n264:       // TODO(mattklein123): Right now the dynamic forward proxy / DNS cache works similar to how\\n265:       //                     logical DNS works, meaning that we only store a single address per\\n266:       //                     resolution. It would not be difficult to also expose strict DNS\\n267:       //                     semantics, meaning the cache would expose multiple addresses and the\\n268:       //                     cluster would create multiple logical hosts based on those addresses.\\n269:       //                     We will leave this is a follow up depending on need.\\n270:       ASSERT(host_info == ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\\\", host);\\n274:       host_map_it->second.logical_host_->setNewAddresses(\\n275:           host_info->address(), host_info->addressList(), dummy_lb_endpoint_);\\n276:       return;\\n277:     }\\n278: \\n279:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\\\", host);\\n280: \\n281:                    info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(),\\n285:                                          host_info->addressList(), dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_,\\n286:                                          dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_))\\n287:                         .first->second.logical_host_;\\n288:   }\\n289: \\n290:   ASSERT(emplaced_host);\\n291:   if (hosts_added std::unique_ptr<Upstream::HostVector>& hosts_added) {\\n242:   Upstream::LogicalHostSharedPtr emplaced_host;\\n243:   {\\n244:     absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\\n245: \\n246:     // NOTE: Right now we allow a DNS cache to be shared between multiple clusters. Though we have\\n247:     // connection/request circuit breakers on the cluster, we don't have any way to control the\\n248:     // maximum hosts on a cluster. We currently assume that host data shared via shared pointer is\\n249:     // a marginal memory cost above that already used by connections and requests, so relying on\\n250:     // connection/request circuit breakers is sufficient. We may have to revisit this in the\\n251:     // future.\\n252:     const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\\n253:     if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\\n254:       // If we only have an address change, we can do that swap inline without any other updates.\\n255:       // The appropriate R/W locking is in place to allow this. The details of this locking are:\\n256:       //  - Hosts are not thread local, they are global.\\n257:       //  - We take a read lock when reading the address and a write lock when changing it.\\n258:       //  - Address updates are very rare.\\n259:       //  - Address reads are only done when a connection is being made and a \\\"real\\\" host\\n260:       //    description is created or the host is queried via the admin endpoint. Both of\\n261:       //    these operations are relatively rare and the read lock is held for a short period\\n262:       //    of time.\\n263:       //\\n264:       // TODO(mattklein123): Right now the dynamic forward proxy / DNS cache works similar to how\\n265:       //                     logical DNS works, meaning that we only store a single address per\\n266:       //                     resolution. It would not be difficult to also expose strict DNS\\n267:       //                     semantics, meaning the cache would expose multiple addresses and the\\n268:       //                     cluster would create multiple logical hosts based on those addresses.\\n269:       //                     We will leave this is a follow up depending on need.\\n270:       ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\\n271:       ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\\n272:              host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\\n273:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\\\", host);\\n274:       host_map_it->second.logical_host_->setNewAddresses(\\n275:           host_info->address(), host_info->addressList(), dummy_lb_endpoint_);\\n276:       return;\\n277:     }\\n278: \\n279:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\\\", host);\\n280: \\n281:     emplaced_host = host_map_\\n282:                         .try_emplace(host, host_info,\\n283:                                      std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\\n284:                                          info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(),\\n285:                                          host_info->addressList(), dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_,\\n286:                                          dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_))\\n287:                         .first->second.logical_host_;\\n288:   }\\n289: \\n290:   ASSERT(emplaced_host);\\n291:   if (hosts_added == nullptr) {\\n292:     hosts_added = std::make_unique<Upstream::HostVector>();\\n293:   }\\n294:   hosts_added->emplace_back(emplaced_host);\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: void return true;\n234:   }\n235:   return false;\n236: }\n237: \n238: void Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(\n239:     absl::string_view host,\n240:     const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info,\n241:     std::unique_ptr<Upstream::HostVector>& hosts_added) {\n242:   Upstream::LogicalHostSharedPtr emplaced_host;\n243:   {\n244:     absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n245: \n246:     // NOTE: Right now we allow a DNS cache to be shared between multiple clusters. Though we have\n247:     // connection/request circuit breakers on the cluster, we don't have any way to control the\n248:     // maximum hosts on a cluster. We currently assume that host data shared via shared pointer is\n249:     // a marginal memory cost above that already used by connections and requests, so relying on\n250:     // connection/request circuit breakers is sufficient. We may have to revisit this in the\n251:     // future.\n252:     const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n253:     if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\n254:       // If we only have an address change, we can do that swap inline without any other updates.\n255:       // The appropriate R/W locking is in place to allow this. The details of this locking are:\n256:       //  - Hosts are not thread local, they are global.\n257:       //  - We take a read lock when reading the address and a write lock when changing it.\n258:       //  - Address updates are very rare.\n259:       //  - Address reads are only done when a connection is being made and a \"real\" host\n260:       //    description is created or the host is queried via the admin endpoint. Both of\n261:       //    these operations are relatively rare and the read lock is held for a short period\n262:       //    of time.\n263:       //\n264:       // TODO(mattklein123): Right now the dynamic forward proxy / DNS cache works similar to how\n265:       //                     logical DNS works, meaning that we only store a single address per\n266:       //                     resolution. It would not be difficult to also expose strict DNS\n267:       //                     semantics, meaning the cache would expose multiple addresses and the\n268:       //                     cluster would create multiple logical hosts based on those addresses.\n269:       //                     We will leave this is a follow up depending on need.\n270:       ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\n271:       ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\n272:              host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\n273:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\", host);\n274:       host_map_it->second.logical_host_->setNewAddresses(\n275:           host_info->address(), host_info->addressList(), dummy_lb_endpoint_);\n276:       return;\n277:     }\n278: \n279:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n280: \n281:     emplaced_host = host_map_\n282:                         .try_emplace(host, host_info,\n283:                                      std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\n284:                                          info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(),\n285:                                          host_info->addressList(), dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_,\n286:                                          dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_))\n287:                         .first->second.logical_host_;\n288:   }\n289: \n290:   ASSERT(emplaced_host);\n291:   if (hosts_added == nullptr) {\n292:     hosts_added = std::make_unique<Upstream::HostVector>();\n293:   }\n294:   hosts_added->emplace_back(emplaced_host);\n295: }\n296: \n297: void Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\n298:     const std::string& host,\n299:     const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n300:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n301: \n302:   std::unique_ptr<Upstream::HostVector> I notice the test doesn't have an `EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(...))` before the address update, but based on the description, that's something we need to add.\n\nLooking at what the fix actually does: instead of updating a host's address in place, it removes the old host and creates a new `LogicalHost` with the new address, then emits both `hosts_removed` and `hosts_added` events to notify the priority set of the change.    return;\n  }\n  // Should remove all sub clusters, otherwise, might be memory leaking.\n  // This lock is useless, just make compiler happy.\n  absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n  for (auto it = cluster_map_.cbegin(); it != cluster_map_.cend();) {\n    auto cluster_name = it->first;\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' removing from cluster_map & cluster manager\", cluster_name);\n    cluster_map_.erase(it++);\n    cm_.removeCluster(cluster_name);\n  }\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::startPreInit() {\n  // If we are attaching to a pre-populated cache we need to initialize our hosts.\n  dns_cache_->iterateHostMap(\n      [&](absl::string_view host, const Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& info) {\n        addOrUpdateHost(host, info);\n      });\n (cluster_it != cluster_map_.end()) {\n    cluster_it->second->touch();\n    return true;\n  }\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' has been removed while touching\", cluster_name);\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::checkIdleSubCluster() {\n  ASSERT(main_thread_dispatcher_.isThreadSafe());\n  {\n    // TODO: try read lock first.\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n    for (auto it = cluster_map_.cbegin(); it != cluster_map_.cend();) {\n      if (it->second->checkIdle()) {\n        auto cluster_name = it->first;\n        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' removing from cluster_map & cluster manager\", cluster_name);\n        cluster_map_.erase(it++);\n        cm_.removeCluster(cluster_name);\n      } else {\n        ++it;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  idle_timer_->enableTimer(sub_cluster_ttl_);\n}\n\nstd::pair<bool, absl::optional<envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster>>\nCluster::createSubClusterConfig(const std::string& cluster_name, const std::string& host,\n                                const int port) {\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&cluster_map_lock_};\n    const auto cluster_it = cluster_map_.find(cluster_name);\n    if (cluster_it != cluster_map_.end()) {\n      cluster_it->second->touch();\n      return std::make_pair(true, absl::nullopt);\n    }\n    if (cluster_map_.size() >= max_sub_clusters_) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' create failed due to max sub cluster limitation\",\n                cluster_name);\n      return std::make_pair(false, absl::nullopt);\n    }\n    cluster_map_.emplace(cluster_name, std::make_shared<ClusterInfo>(cluster_name, *this));\n  }\n\n  // Inherit configuration from the parent DFP cluster.\n  envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster config = orig_cluster_config_;\n\n  // Overwrite the type.\n  config.set_name(cluster_name);\n  config.clear_cluster_type();\n  config.set_lb_policy(sub_cluster_lb_policy_);\n  config.set_type(\n      envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster_DiscoveryType::Cluster_DiscoveryType_STRICT_DNS);\n\n  // Set endpoint.\n  auto load_assignments = config.mutable_load_assignment();\n  load_assignments->set_cluster_name(cluster_name);\n  load_assignments->clear_endpoints();\n\n  auto socket_address = load_assignments->add_endpoints()\n                            ->add_lb_endpoints()\n                            ->mutable_endpoint()\n                            ->mutable_address()\n                            ->mutable_socket_address();\n  socket_address->set_address(host);\n  socket_address->set_port_value(port);\n\n  return std::make_pair(true, absl::make_optional(config));\n}\n\nUpstream::HostConstSharedPtr Cluster::chooseHost(absl::string_view host,\n                                                 Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* context) const {\n  uint16_t default_port = 80;\n  if (info_->transportSocketMatcher().resolve(nullptr).factory_.implementsSecureTransport()) {\n    default_port = 443;\n  }\n\n  const auto host_attributes = Http::Utility::parseAuthority(host);\n  auto dynamic_host = std::string(host_attributes.host_);\n  auto port = host_attributes.port_.value_or(default_port);\n\n  // cluster name is prefix + host + port\n  auto cluster_name = \"DFPCluster:\" + dynamic_host + \":\" + std::to_string(port);\n\n  // try again to get the sub cluster.\n  auto cluster = cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster_name);\n  if (cluster == nullptr) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' get thread local failed, too short ttl?\", cluster_name);\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  return cluster->loadBalancer().chooseHost(context);\n}\n\nCluster::ClusterInfo::ClusterInfo(std::string cluster_name, Cluster& parent)\n    : cluster_name_(cluster_name), parent_(parent) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' ClusterInfo created\", cluster_name_);\n  touch();\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::ClusterInfo::touch() {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' updating last used time\", cluster_name_);\n  last_used_time_ = parent_.time_source_.monotonicTime().time_since_epoch();\n}\n\n// checkIdle run in the main thread.\nbool Cluster::ClusterInfo::checkIdle() {\n  ASSERT(parent_.main_thread_dispatcher_.isThreadSafe());\n\n  const std::chrono::steady_clock::duration now_duration =\n      parent_.main_thread_dispatcher_.timeSource().monotonicTime().time_since_epoch();\n  auto last_used_time = last_used_time_.load();\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' TTL check: now={} last_used={} TTL {}\", cluster_name_,\n            now_duration.count(), last_used_time.count(), parent_.sub_cluster_ttl_.count());\n\n  if ((now_duration - last_used_time) > parent_.sub_cluster_ttl_) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' TTL expired\", cluster_name_);\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(\n    absl::string_view host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_added;\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n\n    // NOTE: Right now we allow a DNS cache to be shared between multiple clusters. Though we have\n    // connection/request circuit breakers on the cluster, we don't have any way to control the\n    // maximum hosts on a cluster. We currently assume that host data shared via shared pointer is\n    // a marginal memory cost above that already used by connections and requests, so relying on\n    // connection/request circuit breakers is sufficient. We may have to revisit this in the\n    // future.\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\n      // If the address has changed, remove the old host and add a new one so that the\n      // cross-priority host map is properly updated with hosts_added and hosts_removed events.\n      //\n      // TODO(mattklein123): Right now the dynamic forward proxy / DNS cache works similar to how\n      //                     logical DNS works, meaning that we only store a single address per\n      //                     resolution. It would not be difficult to also expose strict DNS\n      //                     semantics, meaning the cache would expose multiple addresses and the\n      //                     cluster would create multiple logical hosts based on those addresses.\n      //                     We will leave this is a follow up depending on need.\n      ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\n      ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\n             host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\", host);\n      hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n      host_map_.erase(host_map_it);\n    } else {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n    }\n\n    auto new_host = std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\n        info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(), host_info->addressList(),\n        dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_, dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_);\n    host_map_.emplace(host, HostInfo{host_info, new_host});\n    hosts_added.emplace_back(new_host);\n  }\n\n  updatePriorityState(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\n    const std::string& host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n\n  addOrUpdateHost(host, host_info);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::updatePriorityState(const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added,\n                                  const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed) {\n  Upstream::PriorityStateManager priority_state_manager(*this, local_info_, nullptr);\n  priority_state_manager.initializePriorityFor(dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_);\n  {\n    absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n    for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\n      priority_state_manager.registerHostForPriority(host.second.logical_host_,\n                                                     dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_);\n    }\n  }\n  priority_state_manager.updateClusterPrioritySet(\n      0, std::move(priority_state_manager.priorityState()[0].first), hosts_added, hosts_removed,\n      absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostRemove(const std::string& host) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    ASSERT(host_map_it != host_map_.end());\n    hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n    host_map_.erase(host);\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"removing dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n  }\n  updatePriorityState({}, hosts_removed);\n}\n\nUpstream::HostConstSharedPtr\nCluster::LoadBalancer::chooseHost(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* context) {\n  if (!context) {\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  const Router::StringAccessor* dynamic_host_filter_state = nullptr;\n  if (context->requestStreamInfo()) {\n    dynamic_host_filter_state =\n        context->requestStreamInfo()->filterState().getDataReadOnly<Router::StringAccessor>(\n            DynamicHostFilterStateKey);\n  }\n\n  absl::string_view raw_host;\n  if (dynamic_host_filter_state) {\n    raw_host = dynamic_host_filter_state->asString();\n  } else if (context->downstreamHeaders()) {\n    raw_host = context->downstreamHeaders()->getHostValue();\n  } else if (context->downstreamConnection()) {\n    raw_host = context->downstreamConnection()->requestedServerName();\n  }\n\n  // For host lookup, we need to make sure to match the host of any DNS cache\n  // insert. Two code points currently do DNS cache insert: the http DFP filter,\n  // which inserts for HTTP traffic, and sets port based on the cluster's\n  // security level, and the SNI DFP network filter which sets port based on\n  // stream metadata, or configuration (which is then added as stream metadata).\n  const bool is_secure = cluster_.info()\n                             ->transportSocketMatcher()\n                             .resolve(nullptr)\n                             .factory_.implementsSecureTransport();\n  uint32_t port = is_secure ? 443 : 80;\n  if (context->requestStreamInfo()) {\n    const StreamInfo::UInt32Accessor* dynamic_port_filter_state =\n        context->requestStreamInfo()->filterState().getDataReadOnly<StreamInfo::UInt32Accessor>(\n            DynamicPortFilterStateKey);\n    if (dynamic_port_filter_state != nullptr && dynamic_port_filter_state->value() > 0 &&\n        dynamic_port_filter_state->value() <= 65535) {\n      port = dynamic_port_filter_state->value();\n    }\n  }\n\n  std::string host = Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfo::normalizeHostForDfp(raw_host, port);\n\n  if (host.empty()) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host empty\");\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  if (cluster_.enableSubCluster()) {\n    return cluster_.chooseHost(host, context);\n  }\n\n  {\n    absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&cluster_.host_map_lock_};\n    const auto host_it = cluster_.host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_it == cluster_.host_map_.end()) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host {} not found\", host);\n      return nullptr;\n    } else {\n      if (host_it->second.logical_host_->coarseHealth() == Upstream::Host::Health::Unhealthy) {\n        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host {} is unhealthy\", host);\n        return nullptr;\n      }\n      host_it->second.shared_host_info_->touch();\n      return host_it->second.logical_host_;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nabsl::optional<Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection>\nCluster::LoadBalancer::selectExistingConnection(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* /*context*/,\n absl::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  LookupKey key = {hash_key, *host.address()};\n  auto it = connection_info_map_.find(key);\n  if (it == connection_info_map_.end()) {\n    return absl::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  for TLS.\n  if (!connection.ssl()) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const std::string alpn = connection.nextProtocol();\n  if (alpn != Http::Utility::AlpnNames::get().Http2 &&\n      alpn != *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()};\n  ConnectionInfo info = {&pool, &connection};\n  connection_info_map_[key].push_back(info);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::LoadBalancer::onConnectionDraining(Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::Instance& pool,\n                                                 std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key,\n                                                 const Network::Connection& connection) {\n  const LookupKey key = {hash_key, *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()};\n  connection_info_map_[key].erase(\n      std::remove_if(connection_info_map_[key].begin(), connection_info_map_[key].end(),\n                     [&pool, &connection](const ConnectionInfo& info) {\n                       return (info.pool_ == &pool && info.connection_ == &connection);\n                     }),\n      connection_info_map_[key].end());\n}\n\nabsl::StatusOr<std::pair<Upstream::ClusterImplBaseSharedPtr, parent_.main_thread_dispatcher_.timeSource().monotonicTime().time_since_epoch();\n  auto last_used_time = last_used_time_.load();\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' TTL check: now={} last_used={} TTL {}\", cluster_name_,\n            now_duration.count(), last_used_time.count(), parent_.sub_cluster_ttl_.count());\n\n  if ((now_duration - last_used_time) > parent_.sub_cluster_ttl_) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"cluster='{}' TTL expired\", cluster_name_);\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::addOrUpdateHost(\n    absl::string_view host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_added;\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n\n    // NOTE: Right now we allow a DNS cache to be shared between multiple clusters. Though we have\n    // connection/request circuit breakers on the cluster, we don't have any way to control the\n    // maximum hosts on a cluster. We currently assume that host data shared via shared pointer is\n    // a marginal memory cost above that already used by connections and requests, so relying on\n    // connection/request circuit breakers is sufficient. We may have to revisit this in the\n    // future.\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_map_it != host_map_.end()) {\n      // If the address has changed, remove the old host and add a new one so that the\n      // cross-priority host map is properly updated with hosts_added and hosts_removed events.\n      //\n      // TODO(mattklein123): Right now the dynamic forward proxy / DNS cache works similar to how\n      //                     logical DNS works, meaning that we only store a single address per\n      //                     resolution. It would not be difficult to also expose strict DNS\n      //                     semantics, meaning the cache would expose multiple addresses and the\n      //                     cluster would create multiple logical hosts based on those addresses.\n      //                     We will leave this is a follow up depending on need.\n      ASSERT(host_info == host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_);\n      ASSERT(host_map_it->second.shared_host_info_->address() !=\n             host_map_it->second.logical_host_->address());\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"updating dfproxy cluster host address '{}'\", host);\n      hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n      host_map_.erase(host_map_it);\n    } else {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"adding new dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n    }\n\n    auto new_host = std::make_shared<Upstream::LogicalHost>(\n        info(), std::string{host}, host_info->address(), host_info->addressList(),\n        dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_, dummy_lb_endpoint_, nullptr, time_source_);\n    host_map_.emplace(host, HostInfo{host_info, new_host});\n    hosts_added.emplace_back(new_host);\n  }\n\n  updatePriorityState(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\n    const std::string& host,\n    const Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr& host_info) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Adding host info for {}\", host);\n\n  addOrUpdateHost(host, host_info);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::updatePriorityState(const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added,\n                                  const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed) {\n  Upstream::PriorityStateManager priority_state_manager(*this, local_info_, nullptr);\n  priority_state_manager.initializePriorityFor(dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_);\n  {\n    absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n    for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\n      priority_state_manager.registerHostForPriority(host.second.logical_host_,\n                                                     dummy_locality_lb_endpoint_);\n    }\n  }\n  priority_state_manager.updateClusterPrioritySet(\n      0, std::move(priority_state_manager.priorityState()[0].first), hosts_added, hosts_removed,\n      absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::onDnsHostRemove(const std::string& host) {\n  Upstream::HostVector hosts_removed;\n  {\n    absl::WriterMutexLock lock{&host_map_lock_};\n    const auto host_map_it = host_map_.find(host);\n    ASSERT(host_map_it != host_map_.end());\n    hosts_removed.emplace_back(host_map_it->second.logical_host_);\n    host_map_.erase(host);\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"removing dfproxy cluster host '{}'\", host);\n  }\n  updatePriorityState({}, hosts_removed);\n}\n\nUpstream::HostConstSharedPtr\nCluster::LoadBalancer::chooseHost(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* context) {\n  if (!context) {\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  const Router::StringAccessor* dynamic_host_filter_state = nullptr;\n  if (context->requestStreamInfo()) {\n    dynamic_host_filter_state =\n        context->requestStreamInfo()->filterState().getDataReadOnly<Router::StringAccessor>(\n            DynamicHostFilterStateKey);\n  }\n\n  absl::string_view raw_host;\n  if (dynamic_host_filter_state) {\n    raw_host = dynamic_host_filter_state->asString();\n  } else if (context->downstreamHeaders()) {\n    raw_host = context->downstreamHeaders()->getHostValue();\n  } else if (context->downstreamConnection()) {\n    raw_host = context->downstreamConnection()->requestedServerName();\n  }\n\n  // For host lookup, we need to make sure to match the host of any DNS cache\n  // insert. Two code points currently do DNS cache insert: the http DFP filter,\n  // which inserts for HTTP traffic, and sets port based on the cluster's\n  // security level, and the SNI DFP network filter which sets port based on\n  // stream metadata, or configuration (which is then added as stream metadata).\n  const bool is_secure = cluster_.info()\n                             ->transportSocketMatcher()\n                             .resolve(nullptr)\n                             .factory_.implementsSecureTransport();\n  uint32_t port = is_secure ? 443 : 80;\n  if (context->requestStreamInfo()) {\n    const StreamInfo::UInt32Accessor* dynamic_port_filter_state =\n        context->requestStreamInfo()->filterState().getDataReadOnly<StreamInfo::UInt32Accessor>(\n            DynamicPortFilterStateKey);\n    if (dynamic_port_filter_state != nullptr && dynamic_port_filter_state->value() > 0 &&\n        dynamic_port_filter_state->value() <= 65535) {\n      port = dynamic_port_filter_state->value();\n    }\n  }\n\n  std::string host = Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfo::normalizeHostForDfp(raw_host, port);\n\n  if (host.empty()) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host empty\");\n    return nullptr;\n  }\n\n  if (cluster_.enableSubCluster()) {\n    return cluster_.chooseHost(host, context);\n  }\n\n  {\n    absl::ReaderMutexLock lock{&cluster_.host_map_lock_};\n    const auto host_it = cluster_.host_map_.find(host);\n    if (host_it == cluster_.host_map_.end()) {\n      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host {} not found\", host);\n      return nullptr;\n    } else {\n      if (host_it->second.logical_host_->coarseHealth() == Upstream::Host::Health::Unhealthy) {\n        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"host {} is unhealthy\", host);\n        return nullptr;\n      }\n      host_it->second.shared_host_info_->touch();\n      return host_it->second.logical_host_;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nabsl::optional<Upstream::SelectedPoolAndConnection>\nCluster::LoadBalancer::selectExistingConnection(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* /*context*/,\n                                                const Upstream::Host& host,\n                                                std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key) {\n  const std::string& hostname = host.hostname();\n  if (hostname.empty()) {\n    return absl::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  LookupKey key = {hash_key, *host.address()};\n  auto it = connection_info_map_.find(key);\n  if (it == connection_info_map_.end()) {\n    return absl::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  for }\n  return makeOptRef<Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks>(*this);\n}\n\nvoid Cluster::LoadBalancer::onConnectionOpen(Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::Instance& pool,\n                                             std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key,\n                                             const Network::Connection& connection) {\n  // Only coalesce connections that are over TLS.\n  if (!connection.ssl()) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const std::string alpn = connection.nextProtocol();\n  if (alpn != Http::Utility::AlpnNames::get().Http2 &&\n      alpn != *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()};\n  ConnectionInfo info = {&pool, &connection};\n  connection_info_map_[key].push_back(info);\n}\n\nvoid                     const Network::Connection& connection) {\n  const LookupKey key = {hash_key, *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()};\n  connection_info_map_[key].erase(\n cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n            cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Remove the host, LB will immediately fail to find the host in the map.\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\")));\n}\n\n// Outlier detection\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, OutlierDetection) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:0\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n  makeTestHost(\"host2:0\", \"5.6.7.8\");\n  InSequence s;\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host2:0\", host_map_[\"host2:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host2:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"5.6.7.8:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host2:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Fail outlier check for host1\n  setOutlierFailed(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\")));\n  // \"host2:0\" should not be affected\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host2:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"5.6.7.8:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host2:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Clear outlier check failure for host1, it should be available again\n  clearOutlierFailed(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n}\n\n// Various invalid LB Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerFactory {\npublic:\n  void initialize(const std::string& yaml_config, bool uses_tls) {\n    envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster cluster_config =\n        Upstream::parseClusterFromV3Yaml(yaml_config);\n    envoy::extensions::clusters::dynamic_forward_proxy::v3::ClusterConfig config;\n    Config::Utility::translateOpaqueConfig(cluster_config.cluster_type().typed_config(),\n                                           ProtobufMessage::getStrictValidationVisitor(), config);\n\n    Envoy::Upstream::ClusterFactoryContextImpl factory_context(\n        server_context_, server_context_.cluster_manager_, nullptr, ssl_context_manager_, nullptr,\n        false);\n\n    ON_CALL(server_context_, api()).WillByDefault(testing::ReturnRef(*api_));\n\n    if (uses_tls) {\n      EXPECT_CALL(ssl_context_manager_, createSslClientContext(_, _));\n    }\n    EXPECT_CALL(*dns_cache_manager_, getCache(_));\n    // Below we return a nullptr handle which has no effect on the code under test but isn't\n    // actually correct. It's possible this will have to change in the future.\n    EXPECT_CALL(*dns_cache_manager_->dns_cache_, addUpdateCallbacks_(_))\n        .WillOnce(DoAll(SaveArgAddress(&update_callbacks_), Return(nullptr)));\n    auto cache = dns_cache_manager_->getCache(config.dns_cache_config()).value();\n    cluster_.reset(\n        new Cluster(cluster_config, std::move(cache), config, factory_context, this->get()));\n    thread_aware_lb_ = std::make_unique<Cluster::ThreadAwareLoadBalancer>(*cluster_);\n    lb_factory_ = thread_aware_lb_->factory();\n    refreshLb();\n\n    ON_CALL(lb_context_, downstreamHeaders()).WillByDefault(Return(&downstream_headers_));\n    ON_CALL(connection_, streamInfo()).WillByDefault(ReturnRef(stream_info_));\n    ON_CALL(lb_context_, requestStreamInfo()).WillByDefault(Return(&stream_info_));\n    ON_CALL(lb_context_, downstreamConnection()).WillByDefault(Return(&connection_));\n\n    member_update_cb_ = cluster_->prioritySet().addMemberUpdateCb(\n        [this](const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added,\n               const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed) -> void {\n          onMemberUpdateCb(hosts_added, hosts_removed);\n        });\n\n    absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsHostInfoSharedPtr>\n        existing_hosts;\n    for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\n      existing_hosts.emplace(host.first, host.second);\n    }\n    EXPECT_CALL(*dns_cache_manager_->dns_cache_, iterateHostMap(_)).WillOnce(Invoke([&](auto cb) {\n      for (const auto& host : host_map_) {\n        cb(host.first, host.second);\n      }\n    }));\n    if (!existing_hosts.empty()) {\n      EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0))).Times(existing_hosts.size());\n    }\n    cluster_->initialize([] {});\n  }\n\n  Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCacheManagerSharedPtr get() override {\n    return dns_cache_manager_;\n  }\n\n  void makeTestHost(const std::string& host, const std::string& address) {\n    EXPECT_TRUE(host_map_.find(host) == host_map_.end());\n    host_map_[host] = std::make_shared<Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsHostInfo>();\n    host_map_[host]->address_ = Network::Utility::parseInternetAddress(address);\n\n    // Allow touch() to still be strict.\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], address()).Times(AtLeast(0));\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], addressList()).Times(AtLeast(0));\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], isIpAddress()).Times(AtLeast(0));\n    EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[host], resolvedHost()).Times(AtLeast(0));\n  }\n\n  void updateTestHostAddress(const std::string& host, const std::string& address) {\n    EXPECT_FALSE(host_map_.find(host) == host_map_.end());\n    host_map_[host]->address_ = Network::Utility::parseInternetAddress(address);\n  }\n\n  void refreshLb() { lb_ = lb_factory_->create(lb_params_); }\n\n  Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext* setHostAndReturnContext(const std::string& host) {\n    downstream_headers_.remove(\":authority\");\n    downstream_headers_.addCopy(\":authority\", host);\n    return &lb_context_;\n  }\n\n  Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext* setFilterStateHostAndReturnContext(const std::string& host) {\n    StreamInfo::FilterState& filter_state =\n        const_cast<StreamInfo::FilterState&>(lb_context_.requestStreamInfo()->filterState());\n\n    filter_state.setData(\n        \"envoy.upstream.dynamic_host\", std::make_shared<Router::StringAccessorImpl>(host),\n        StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable, StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Connection,\n        StreamInfo::StreamSharingMayImpactPooling::SharedWithUpstreamConnection);\n\n    return &lb_context_;\n  }\n\n  void setOutlierFailed(const std::string& host) {\n    for (auto& h : cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()) {\n      if (h->hostname() == host) {\n        h->healthFlagSet(Upstream::Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK);\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  void clearOutlierFailed(const std::string& host) {\n    for (auto& h : cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()) {\n      if (h->hostname() == host) {\n        h->healthFlagClear(Upstream::Host::HealthFlag::FAILED_OUTLIER_CHECK);\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  MOCK_METHOD(void, onMemberUpdateCb,\n              (const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_added, const Upstream::HostVector& hosts_removed));\n\n  NiceMock<Server::Configuration::MockServerFactoryContext> server_context_;\n  Stats::TestUtil::TestStore& stats_store_ = server_context_.store_;\n  Api::ApiPtr api_{Api::createApiForTest(stats_store_)};\n  Ssl::MockContextManager ssl_context_manager_;\n\n  std::shared_ptr<Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsCacheManager> dns_cache_manager_{\n      new Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsCacheManager()};\n  std::shared_ptr<Cluster> cluster_;\n  Upstream::ThreadAwareLoadBalancerPtr thread_aware_lb_;\n  Upstream::LoadBalancerFactorySharedPtr lb_factory_;\n  Upstream::LoadBalancerPtr lb_;\n  NiceMock<Upstream::MockLoadBalancerContext> lb_context_;\n  Http::TestRequestHeaderMapImpl downstream_headers_;\n  Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::DnsCache::UpdateCallbacks* update_callbacks_{};\n  absl::flat_hash_map<std::string,\n                      std::shared_ptr<Extensions::Common::DynamicForwardProxy::MockDnsHostInfo>>\n      host_map_;\n  Envoy::Common::CallbackHandlePtr member_update_cb_;\n  NiceMock<StreamInfo::MockStreamInfo> stream_info_;\n  NiceMock<Network::MockConnection> connection_;\n\n  // Just use this as parameters of create() method but thread aware load balancer will not use it.\n  NiceMock<Upstream::MockPrioritySet> worker_priority_set_;\n  Upstream::LoadBalancerParams lb_params_{worker_priority_set_, {}};\n\n  const std::string sub_cluster_yaml_config_ = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    sub_clusters_config:\n      max_sub_clusters: 1024\n)EOF\";\n\n  const std::string default_yaml_config_ = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    dns_cache_config:\n      name: foo\n      dns_lookup_family: AUTO\n)EOF\";\n\n  const std::string coalesce_connection_config_ = R\"EOF(\nname: name\nconnect_timeout: 0.25s\ncluster_type:\n  name: dynamic_forward_proxy\n  typed_config:\n    \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.clusters.dynamic_forward_proxy.v3.ClusterConfig\n    allow_coalesced_connections: true\n    dns_cache_config:\n      name: foo\n      dns_lookup_family: AUTO\n)EOF\";\n};\n\n// createSubClusterConfig twice.\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, CreateSubClusterConfig) {\n  initialize(sub_cluster_yaml_config_, false);\n\n  const std::string cluster_name = \"fake_cluster_name\";\n  const std::string host = \"localhost\";\n  const int port = 80;\n  std::pair<bool, absl::optional<envoy::config::cluster::v3::Cluster>> sub_cluster_pair =\n      cluster_->createSubClusterConfig(cluster_name, host, port);\n  EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.first);\n  EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.second.has_value());\n\n  // create again, already exists\n  sub_cluster_pair = cluster_->createSubClusterConfig(cluster_name, host, port);\n  EXPECT_EQ(true, sub_cluster_pair.first);\n  EXPECT_EQ(false, sub_cluster_pair.second.has_value());\n}\n\n// Basic flow of the cluster including adding hosts and removing them.\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, BasicFlow) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:0\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n  InSequence s;\n\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"\")));\n\n  // Verify no host LB cases.\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"foo\")));\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->peekAnotherHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"foo\")));\n\n  // LB will immediately resolve host1.\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // After changing the address, LB will immediately resolve the new address with a refresh.\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(1)));\n  updateTestHostAddress(\"host1:0\", \"2.3.4.5\");\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n            cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts()[0]->address()->asString());\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"2.3.4.5:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Remove the host, LB will immediately fail to find the host in the map.\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(0), SizeIs(1)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostRemove(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, cluster_->prioritySet().hostSetsPerPriority()[0]->hosts().size());\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\")));\n}\n\n// Outlier detection\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, OutlierDetection) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:0\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n  makeTestHost(\"host2:0\", \"5.6.7.8\");\n  InSequence s;\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host2:0\", host_map_[\"host2:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host2:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"5.6.7.8:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host2:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Fail outlier check for host1\n  setOutlierFailed(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\")));\n  // \"host2:0\" should not be affected\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host2:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"5.6.7.8:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host2:0\"))->address()->asString());\n\n  // Clear outlier check failure for host1, it should be available again\n  clearOutlierFailed(\"host1:0\");\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n}\n\n// Various invalid LB context permutations in case the cluster is used outside of HTTP.\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, InvalidLbContext) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  ON_CALL(lb_context_, downstreamHeaders()).WillByDefault(Return(nullptr));\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(&lb_context_));\n  EXPECT_EQ(nullptr, lb_->chooseHost(nullptr));\n}\n\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, FilterStateHostOverride) {\n  initialize(default_yaml_config_, false);\n  makeTestHost(\"host1:0\", \"1.2.3.4\");\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(*this, onMemberUpdateCb(SizeIs(1), SizeIs(0)));\n  update_callbacks_->onDnsHostAddOrUpdate(\"host1:0\", host_map_[\"host1:0\"]);\n  EXPECT_CALL(*host_map_[\"host1:0\"], touch());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"1.2.3.4:0\",\n            lb_->chooseHost(setFilterStateHostAndReturnContext(\"host1:0\"))->address()->asString());\n}\n\n// Verify cluster attaches to a populated cache.\nTEST_F(ClusterTest, PopulatedCache) {\n The scheduler is reset by default, and is rebuilt only if we have\n610:   // locality weights (i.e. using EDS) and there is at least one eligible host in this priority.\n611:   //\n612:   // We omit building a scheduler when there are zero eligible hosts in the priority as\n613:   // all the localities will have zero effective weight. At selection time, we'll either select\n614:   // from a different scheduler or there will be no available hosts in the priority. At that point\n615:   // we'll rely on other mechanisms such as panic mode to select a host, none of which rely on the\n616:   // scheduler.\n617:   //\n618:   // TODO(htuch): if the underlying locality index ->\n619:   // envoy::config::core::v3::Locality hasn't changed in hosts_/healthy_hosts_/degraded_hosts_, we\n620:   // could just update locality_weight_ without rebuilding.             const HostsPerLocality& all_hosts_per_locality,\\n711:                                             const LocalityWeights& locality_weights,\\n712:
